name: Teal Hanging Planter with Ring
description: Bring the outdoors in with this stunning deep teal glazed hanging planter, mounted on a sleek circular black metal frame. The rich, glossy glaze shifts between deep ocean teal and midnight blue depending on the light — no two angles look the same. The 12-inch ring frame adds a modern, sculptural silhouette that transforms any wall or ceiling hook into an art installation. Perfect for trailing plants like golden pothos, string of hearts, or burro's tail. Each piece is wheel-thrown by hand, dipped in our signature teal glaze, and kiln-fired to 2,200°F for lasting durability. Because it's handmade, the glaze pattern on your planter will be entirely unique — a true one-of-a-kind piece you won't find anywhere else.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Solid brass hanging hook with swivel mount
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6 (2,200°F)
Lead-free, food-safe glaze in deep teal
Drainage hole with removable silicone plug
Total hanging weight capacity: 8 lbs (plant + soil + water)
Handmade — each piece has unique glaze variations

name: Mint & Blue Gradient Ring Planter
description: This planter is a watercolor painting frozen in clay. Starting with a soft, seafoam mint at the rim, the glaze flows downward through layers of cerulean blue before settling into warm, earthy brown tones at the base — like a coastal sunset captured in ceramic. Mounted on a minimalist circular metal ring, it creates a striking visual focal point whether hung in a bright kitchen window, a cozy reading nook, or on a covered patio. The three-tone gradient is achieved through a meticulous layered glazing process: each color is applied by hand, one at a time, before the piece enters the kiln. The result is a seamless transition that's impossible to replicate — your planter will be the only one in the world with this exact color story.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Three-layer glaze technique: mint, cerulean blue, warm brown
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6 (2,200°F)
Lead-free, food-safe glazes throughout
Drainage hole with removable silicone plug
UV-resistant glaze — safe for indirect outdoor display
Handmade — no two gradient patterns are identical

name: Dark Teal Hanging Planter — Garden Edition
description: There's something magical about a deep, moody glaze catching dappled garden light — and this planter was made for exactly that moment. The dark teal surface has a quiet depth to it: look closely and you'll see subtle shifts from near-black to forest green to a warm bronze shimmer where the glaze pools thickest. Shown here bursting with trailing string of hearts and cascading vines, it's a planter that becomes more beautiful as your plants grow into it. The circular metal ring frame gives it that modern, gallery-worthy presentation, while the generous 6-inch bowl provides plenty of room for root growth. Hang it from a porch beam, a shepherd's hook in the garden, or a ceiling hook indoors — it works everywhere.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Dark teal glaze with bronze undertone variations
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6
Drainage hole for healthy root systems
Weather-resistant for covered outdoor spaces
Brass hanging hook included
Handmade — subtle glaze shifts are unique to each piece

name: Deep Teal Garden Ring Planter
description: Photographed in a sun-dappled garden with soft bokeh light filtering through the trees behind it, this planter practically glows. The deep teal glaze has a mirror-like quality — glossy, rich, and full of movement. In direct light it reads as a vivid jewel-tone teal; in shade it deepens to near-emerald. The contrast against lush green foliage is absolutely stunning, which is why this piece was specifically designed for garden display. The minimalist black metal ring keeps the visual focus on the pottery and your plants, creating a floating, sculptural effect. Whether you fill it with a lush fern, a dramatic spider plant, or a simple trailing ivy, this planter elevates everything it holds.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Deep teal high-gloss glaze with emerald undertones
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6
Drainage hole with removable plug
Powder-coated metal ring — rust-resistant for outdoor use
Brass swivel hook for easy 360° rotation
Handmade — glaze depth varies beautifully per piece

name: Dark Green Studio Ring Planter
description: Sophisticated doesn't begin to cover it. This planter's dark green-black glaze has the kind of depth you'd expect from a piece of fine jewelry — a rich, almost liquid-looking surface that shifts between deep forest green and polished obsidian depending on the angle. Studio-photographed against a clean white background to show every nuance of the handmade glaze work, this is the planter for someone who wants their home décor to feel intentional, elevated, and unlike anything mass-produced. The circular metal ring frame adds a geometric counterpoint to the organic, hand-shaped bowl. It's the kind of piece that guests notice immediately and ask about. Pair it with a striking plant — a silvery Scindapsus or a dark burgundy Tradescantia — and you've got a living art installation.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Dark green-black high-gloss glaze with obsidian shifts
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6 (2,200°F)
Lead-free, food-safe glaze
Drainage hole with removable silicone plug
Gift-ready — ships in custom protective packaging
Handmade — each piece is a unique work of art

name: Orange Drip-Glaze Vase
description: This is the piece that stops people mid-sentence. A vibrant, sun-kissed orange body — bold, warm, and unapologetically joyful — crowned with a cascade of sage-green glaze that drips and flows over the rim like moss on a canyon wall. The contrast is electric: warm meets cool, bright meets earthy, smooth meets organic. Standing approximately 8 inches tall with a wide mouth, it's equally stunning holding a bouquet of fresh tulips, dried pampas grass, or absolutely nothing at all. The drip pattern is created by applying a thick layer of sage glaze to the rim before firing — gravity and heat do the rest, meaning no two drip patterns will ever be the same. This is the vase that becomes the centerpiece of whatever room it lives in. Shown here on a sunlit garden table with white tulips — but honestly, it doesn't need flowers to be a showstopper.
details: Approximately 8" tall x 5" diameter at widest point
Wide 3.5" mouth opening — perfect for bouquets
Vibrant orange body with sage-green drip glaze from rim
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6
Watertight — holds fresh flowers and water without leaking
Lead-free, food-safe glazes
Weighted base for stability with tall arrangements
Handmade — drip pattern is unique, created by gravity during firing

name: Orange Footed Drip Planter
description: Small but mighty, this three-footed planter packs an enormous amount of personality into a compact form. The vibrant orange body radiates warmth and energy, while the sage-green drip glaze cascading from the rim adds an organic, almost whimsical touch — like icing dripping down the side of a cake. The three rustic feet aren't just decorative: they elevate the planter off the surface for proper air circulation and drainage, keeping your plants healthier and your furniture protected. At 5 inches tall and 5 inches wide, it's the perfect size for a desk succulent, a kitchen windowsill herb, or a bedside snake plant (shown here with a gorgeous Sansevieria). The proportions are wonderfully satisfying — sturdy enough to feel substantial, compact enough to fit anywhere. This is the piece that makes people say 'Where did you get that?'
details: Approximately 5" tall x 5" diameter (including feet)
Three hand-shaped feet — elevates plant 1" off surface
Orange body with sage drip-glaze rim detail
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6
Drainage hole in base for healthy root systems
Lead-free, food-safe glaze
Felt pads included for furniture protection
Handmade — foot shape and drip pattern unique to each piece

name: Teal Ring Planter — Flower Edition
description: Some planters hold plants. This one puts on a show. Photographed overflowing with vibrant pink petunias, trailing lobelia, and lush sweet potato vine, this teal ring planter demonstrates what happens when handmade pottery meets serious flower power. The earthy teal glaze — with its warm brown undertones — provides a grounding, natural backdrop that lets even the most colorful blooms take center stage without visual competition. The 12-inch circular metal ring frame creates a halo effect around the arrangement, adding structure and drama. Whether you plant it up for a summer porch display, hang it as a living wreath alternative, or use it year-round with trailing houseplants, this is a planter that earns its place as a conversation piece. The teal glaze deepens with age and exposure to natural light, becoming even more beautiful over time.
details: Approximately 6" diameter x 5" tall planter bowl
12" diameter circular black metal ring frame included
Earthy teal glaze with warm brown undertones
Wheel-thrown stoneware, kiln-fired to cone 6
Extra-deep bowl for generous root space
Drainage hole for outdoor flower plantings
Powder-coated ring — weather-resistant for porch/patio
Handmade — glaze color deepens beautifully with age
